title: Macrame hanging basket
subtitle: A knotted plant holder for a 12-14 cm pot using square knots and a gathering knot finish
excerpt: A macramé hanging basket holds a potted plant at window or ceiling height using a gathered knot structure suspended from a ring. The basket is made from eight folded cords knotted in alternating square knots, with a gathering knot at the base that supports the pot. This entry covers a single pot holder sized for a standard 12-14 cm terracotta pot.
sourceNotes: Macramé plant hangers are one of the most widely practiced contemporary macramé forms; the alternating square knot basket has been continuously produced since the 1960s macramé revival. Method follows standard UK workshop instruction.

at the base. The pot sits in the cradle formed by this base knot. The basket is attached to a metal or wooden ring at the top which takes the weight when hung.

items.name: 4 mm single-strand cotton macramé cord, natural
Metal or wooden ring, 5-6 cm diameter

content.text: Step 1: Mount cords on the ring

Cut 8 cords, each 350 cm long. Fold each in half and attach to the ring with a lark's head knot, distributing them evenly around the ring. You will have 16 working cord ends. Secure the ring to a hook or hanging point at a comfortable working height.
Step 2: Work the top gathering knot

Approximately 15 cm below the ring, work a gathering knot around all 16 cords. Wrap a separate 30 cm length of cord around the bundle 6-8 times, then thread the end through the wraps and pull tight. This consolidates the cords into a single column below the ring.
Step 3: Work the alternating square knot section

Separate the 16 cords into 4 groups of 4. Work one square knot on each group, 5 cm below the gathering knot. In the next row, drop 2 cords from adjacent groups and work square knots on the new groupings. Repeat for 3-4 alternating rows, depending on the depth of the pot.
Step 4: Base gathering knot and fringe

Collect all 16 cords together approximately 8 cm below the last knot row. Tie a gathering knot around the full bundle. This forms the base cradle that the pot will rest on. Trim the remaining cord ends to 15-20 cm and unravel them to form a fluffy tassel below the basket.

items.fix: Measure the distance from the gathering knot to each square knot row before tying. Mark the position with tape on the cord bundle before knotting.
items.cause: Uneven tension in the alternating square knot section, so one side of the basket is longer than the other
items.problem: The pot sits at an angle in the basket

items.fix: Wrap the gathering knot at least 8-10 times and thread the end through the wraps twice. A securing knot at the end of the wrap thread prevents loosening under load.
items.cause: Gathering knot wraps not secured tightly enough, or too few wraps
items.problem: The base gathering knot slips when the pot is placed in the basket
